intent: Build a simple Python task management library (tasklib). It is not a production system. Its purpose is to validate that the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline can close a complete dependency chain — from documentation through scaffold to working code — with real merges at each step.
scope_statement:
Build a minimal Python task management library (tasklib) as defined in TRD-TASKLIB, consisting of a documentation set (README, ARCHITECTURE, API reference), a Python package scaffold under src/tasklib/ with models/ and storage/ subpackages, a Task dataclass with TaskStatus enumeration (FR-1 through FR-7), an InMemoryTaskStore with add/get/list/complete/delete operations (FR-8 through FR-14), a CLI entry point supporting add/list/complete commands (FR-15 through FR-19), and a package root re-exporting the public API (FR-20). The purpose is to validate the Crafted Dev Agent build pipeline's ability to close a complete dependency chain — docs → scaffold → model → storage → CLI — with real merges at each step.

Operator on 'Should the dependency chain be decomposed into exactly 5 PRs':
build the PRDs in the way that works best for code development

Operator on 'For the Task identifier (FR-1), should we use UUID4 strings ':
use the PRD process to determine and resolve any gaps. Use best practices.

Operator on 'Should tests be included in each component PR alongside the ':
use the PRD process to determine and resolve any gaps. Use best practices.

Operator on 'Is there a target Python version (e.g., 3.10+ for match stat':
Python 3.11+ 3.14.0

prd_plan.title: Documentation Baseline — README, ARCHITECTURE, and API Reference
Package Scaffold — src/tasklib/ Directory Tree with pyproject.toml
Task Model — Task Dataclass and TaskStatus Enumeration
InMemoryTaskStore — Storage Layer with CRUD Operations
CLI Entry Point and Package Root Public API Re-exports

1.title: Add README.md — library purpose, structure, and usage example
1.summary: Define the TaskStatus enumeration as a StrEnum (Python 3.11+) with exactly three members: PENDING='pending', IN_PROGRESS='in_progress', DONE='done'. This replaces the placeholder content in src/tasklib/models/task.py with the enumeration only — the Task dataclass is delivered in the next PR to keep the diff minimal and independently reviewable.

2.title: Add ARCHITECTURE.md — dependency chain and design constraints
2.summary: Add the Task dataclass to src/tasklib/models/task.py alongside the existing TaskStatus enum. Task is a @dataclass with fields: id (str, default_factory=uuid4().hex), title (str, required), status (TaskStatus, default PENDING), created_at (float, default_factory=time.time). __post_init__ validates title is non-empty and non-whitespace-only, raising ValueError. to_dict() returns a plain dict with status serialized as its string value. from_dict() is a classmethod that reconstructs a Task from a dict, coercing the status string through TaskStatus and raising ValueError for invalid status strings.

3.title: Add API.md — public API reference for Task, TaskStatus, InMemoryTaskStore, and CLI
3.summary: Update src/tasklib/models/__init__.py to import and re-export Task and TaskStatus with an explicit __all__ = ['Task', 'TaskStatus']. This enables downstream consumers (store, CLI, package root) to use `from tasklib.models import Task, TaskStatus` as the canonical import path.

4.title: Validate documentation set — structural and content lint tests
4.summary: Creates three placeholder modules: src/tasklib/models/task.py, src/tasklib/storage/store.py, and src/tasklib/cli.py. Each file contains only a module-level docstring describing its future purpose. No classes, functions, imports, or executable code are defined. This separates package structure from leaf module content.

5.title: Add scaffold import verification tests
5.summary: Creates tests/__init__.py and tests/test_scaffold.py verifying: (1) 'import tasklib' succeeds, (2) 'import tasklib.models' succeeds, (3) 'import tasklib.storage' succeeds, (4) 'import tasklib.cli' succeeds, (5) 'import tasklib.models.task' succeeds, (6) 'import tasklib.storage.store' succeeds, (7) none of the imported modules expose unexpected public names.
